ABCD_SubMod_StatAnalysis_020326

Felicia Hardi

2026-02-03

Load packages

library(broom)
library(lavaan)
library(cluster)
library(dplyr)
library(emmeans)
library(factoextra)
library(ggeffects)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(grid)
library(Hmisc)
library(interactions)
library(kableExtra)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(knitr)
library(magrittr)
library(NbClust)
library(psych)
library(purrr)
library(table1)
library(tidyverse)
library(viridis)

Change in BPM across time, by clusters

# test if the change in bpm differs by groups, with sex modeled explicitly

# internalizing
mod1 = (lmer(
    bpm_int ~ (age_c + I(age_c^2)) * clust * sex +
        (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) +
        pub_z + changeage,
    data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long,
    na.action = na.exclude,
    weights = weights
))
anova(mod1)
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                      Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF  F value    Pr(>F)    
## age_c                160.27  160.27     1 41074 203.2768 < 2.2e-16 ***
## I(age_c^2)           141.48  141.48     1 35741 179.4438 < 2.2e-16 ***
## clust                147.99   74.00     2 16580  93.8544 < 2.2e-16 ***
## sex                   51.82   51.82     1 16823  65.7323 5.517e-16 ***
## pub_z                 18.24   18.24     1 13076  23.1366 1.526e-06 ***
## changeage              8.30    8.30     1 35389  10.5296  0.001176 ** 
## age_c:clust           22.17   11.09     2 40873  14.0619 7.854e-07 ***
## I(age_c^2):clust       2.36    1.18     2 35750   1.4959  0.224051    
## age_c:sex            457.00  457.00     1 40840 579.6383 < 2.2e-16 ***
## I(age_c^2):sex        13.34   13.34     1 35748  16.9216 3.904e-05 ***
## clust:sex             10.15    5.08     2 17699   6.4372  0.001605 ** 
## age_c:clust:sex       25.71   12.85     2 40875  16.3033 8.363e-08 ***
## I(age_c^2):clust:sex   5.73    2.87     2 35752   3.6370  0.026342 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# B) Estimated means at mean age (cluster differences at age_c = 0)
emm_int <- emmeans(mod1, ~ clust, at = list(age_c = 0), weights = "proportional")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 43581' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 43581)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 43581' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 43581)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
summary(emm_int)
##  clust emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1       1.51 0.0364 Inf      1.44      1.58
##  2       2.13 0.0511 Inf      2.03      2.23
##  3       1.74 0.0491 Inf      1.65      1.84
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95
pairs(emm_int, adjust = "fdr")
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -0.619 0.0461 Inf -13.429 <0.0001
##  clust1 - clust3   -0.237 0.0450 Inf  -5.259 <0.0001
##  clust2 - clust3    0.383 0.0566 Inf   6.755 <0.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# C) Simple age slopes by cluster within sex, at age_c = 0 (and maybe -1, +1 if reported)
slopes_int <- emtrends(mod1, ~ clust | sex * age_c, var = "age_c", at = list(age_c = c(-1, 0, 1)))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 43581' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 43581)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 43581' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 43581)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
test(slopes_int, adjust = "fdr")
## sex = 1, age_c = -1:
##  clust age_c.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1         -0.2091 0.0269 Inf  -7.778 <0.0001
##  2         -0.2558 0.0511 Inf  -5.002 <0.0001
##  3         -0.2151 0.0474 Inf  -4.540 <0.0001
## 
## sex = 2, age_c = -1:
##  clust age_c.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1          0.1055 0.0273 Inf   3.863  0.0002
##  2          0.3897 0.0516 Inf   7.559 <0.0001
##  3         -0.0865 0.0469 Inf  -1.843  0.0654
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  0:
##  clust age_c.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1         -0.0729 0.0150 Inf  -4.878 <0.0001
##  2         -0.1019 0.0286 Inf  -3.562  0.0004
##  3         -0.1002 0.0272 Inf  -3.691  0.0003
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  0:
##  clust age_c.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1          0.3537 0.0157 Inf  22.565 <0.0001
##  2          0.5575 0.0302 Inf  18.483 <0.0001
##  3          0.2595 0.0268 Inf   9.694 <0.0001
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  1:
##  clust age_c.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1          0.0633 0.0253 Inf   2.503  0.0370
##  2          0.0519 0.0490 Inf   1.060  0.4339
##  3          0.0146 0.0484 Inf   0.302  0.7623
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  1:
##  clust age_c.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1          0.6019 0.0273 Inf  22.029 <0.0001
##  2          0.7253 0.0526 Inf  13.784 <0.0001
##  3          0.6054 0.0480 Inf  12.612 <0.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
pairs(slopes_int, adjust = "fdr")
## sex = 1, age_c = -1:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  0.04664 0.0577 Inf   0.808  0.8396
##  clust1 - clust3  0.00601 0.0544 Inf   0.110  0.9121
##  clust2 - clust3 -0.04063 0.0697 Inf  -0.583  0.8396
## 
## sex = 2, age_c = -1:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2 -0.28416 0.0583 Inf  -4.878 <0.0001
##  clust1 - clust3  0.19199 0.0542 Inf   3.541  0.0004
##  clust2 - clust3  0.47615 0.0697 Inf   6.836 <0.0001
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  0:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  0.02901 0.0320 Inf   0.906  0.5609
##  clust1 - clust3  0.02731 0.0307 Inf   0.889  0.5609
##  clust2 - clust3 -0.00170 0.0392 Inf  -0.043  0.9655
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  0:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2 -0.20376 0.0337 Inf  -6.048 <0.0001
##  clust1 - clust3  0.09426 0.0307 Inf   3.069  0.0021
##  clust2 - clust3  0.29802 0.0401 Inf   7.433 <0.0001
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  1:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  0.01139 0.0548 Inf   0.208  0.8354
##  clust1 - clust3  0.04862 0.0543 Inf   0.895  0.8354
##  clust2 - clust3  0.03723 0.0686 Inf   0.543  0.8354
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  1:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2 -0.12336 0.0590 Inf  -2.092  0.1093
##  clust1 - clust3 -0.00347 0.0549 Inf  -0.063  0.9496
##  clust2 - clust3  0.11989 0.0710 Inf   1.689  0.1368
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# externalizing
mod2 = (lmer(
    bpm_ext ~ (age_c + I(age_c^2)) * clust * sex +
        (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) +
        pub_z + changeage,
    data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long,
    na.action = na.exclude,
    weights = weights
))
anova(mod2)
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                       Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF  F value    Pr(>F)    
## age_c                 72.100  72.100     1 39734 125.5070 < 2.2e-16 ***
## I(age_c^2)            26.995  26.995     1 34974  46.9900 7.253e-12 ***
## clust                200.945 100.472     2 16150 174.8946 < 2.2e-16 ***
## sex                   13.614  13.614     1 17037  23.6985 1.137e-06 ***
## pub_z                  3.756   3.756     1 13638   6.5390  0.010564 *  
## changeage             30.581  30.581     1 34705  53.2327 3.026e-13 ***
## age_c:clust            4.950   2.475     2 39563   4.3087  0.013458 *  
## I(age_c^2):clust       1.503   0.751     2 34981   1.3078  0.270419    
## age_c:sex             91.671  91.671     1 39525 159.5735 < 2.2e-16 ***
## I(age_c^2):sex         4.580   4.580     1 34981   7.9724  0.004752 ** 
## clust:sex              0.331   0.166     2 17813   0.2884  0.749464    
## age_c:clust:sex        7.083   3.542     2 39566   6.1651  0.002103 ** 
## I(age_c^2):clust:sex   2.680   1.340     2 34983   2.3321  0.097102 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# B) Estimated means at mean age (cluster differences at age_c = 0)
emm_ext <- emmeans(mod2, ~ clust, at = list(age_c = 0), weights = "proportional")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 43331' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 43331)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 43331' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 43331)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
summary(emm_ext)
##  clust emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1       1.66 0.0334 Inf      1.59      1.72
##  2       2.42 0.0468 Inf      2.32      2.51
##  3       2.03 0.0450 Inf      1.94      2.12
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95
pairs(emm_ext, adjust = "fdr")
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -0.759 0.0421 Inf -18.027 <0.0001
##  clust1 - clust3   -0.375 0.0412 Inf  -9.095 <0.0001
##  clust2 - clust3    0.384 0.0518 Inf   7.415 <0.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# C) Simple age slopes by cluster within sex, at age_c = 0 (and maybe -1, +1 if reported)
slopes_ext <- emtrends(mod2, ~ clust | sex * age_c, var = "age_c", at = list(age_c = c(-1, 0, 1)))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 43331' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 43331)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 43331' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 43331)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
test(slopes_ext, adjust = "fdr")
## sex = 1, age_c = -1:
##  clust age_c.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1        -0.09212 0.0231 Inf  -3.996  0.0002
##  2        -0.04240 0.0439 Inf  -0.966  0.3338
##  3        -0.04041 0.0405 Inf  -0.998  0.3338
## 
## sex = 2, age_c = -1:
##  clust age_c.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1         0.03690 0.0234 Inf   1.580  0.1710
##  2         0.18824 0.0446 Inf   4.220 <0.0001
##  3         0.04414 0.0405 Inf   1.091  0.2754
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  0:
##  clust age_c.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1        -0.00208 0.0129 Inf  -0.161  0.8721
##  2        -0.01240 0.0247 Inf  -0.503  0.8721
##  3        -0.00964 0.0234 Inf  -0.413  0.8721
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  0:
##  clust age_c.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1         0.14767 0.0135 Inf  10.937 <0.0001
##  2         0.26899 0.0260 Inf  10.333 <0.0001
##  3         0.21348 0.0231 Inf   9.231 <0.0001
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  1:
##  clust age_c.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1         0.08797 0.0217 Inf   4.045  0.0002
##  2         0.01761 0.0419 Inf   0.420  0.6743
##  3         0.02112 0.0416 Inf   0.508  0.6743
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  1:
##  clust age_c.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1         0.25844 0.0234 Inf  11.032 <0.0001
##  2         0.34974 0.0449 Inf   7.789 <0.0001
##  3         0.38281 0.0415 Inf   9.226 <0.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
pairs(slopes_ext, adjust = "fdr")
## sex = 1, age_c = -1:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2 -0.04972 0.0495 Inf  -1.004  0.4729
##  clust1 - clust3 -0.05172 0.0465 Inf  -1.112  0.4729
##  clust2 - clust3 -0.00199 0.0596 Inf  -0.033  0.9733
## 
## sex = 2, age_c = -1:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2 -0.15133 0.0503 Inf  -3.010  0.0078
##  clust1 - clust3 -0.00723 0.0467 Inf  -0.155  0.8768
##  clust2 - clust3  0.14410 0.0602 Inf   2.395  0.0250
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  0:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  0.01032 0.0276 Inf   0.374  0.9350
##  clust1 - clust3  0.00756 0.0265 Inf   0.286  0.9350
##  clust2 - clust3 -0.00276 0.0338 Inf  -0.082  0.9350
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  0:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2 -0.12132 0.0291 Inf  -4.174 <0.0001
##  clust1 - clust3 -0.06580 0.0265 Inf  -2.481  0.0197
##  clust2 - clust3  0.05551 0.0346 Inf   1.603  0.1088
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  1:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  0.07036 0.0469 Inf   1.499  0.2282
##  clust1 - clust3  0.06684 0.0467 Inf   1.432  0.2282
##  clust2 - clust3 -0.00352 0.0588 Inf  -0.060  0.9523
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  1:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2 -0.09130 0.0504 Inf  -1.813  0.1048
##  clust1 - clust3 -0.12437 0.0474 Inf  -2.625  0.0260
##  clust2 - clust3 -0.03307 0.0609 Inf  -0.543  0.5873
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# is the quad better fit than the linear model? had to remove weights bc of convergence
lin1 = (lmer(
    bpm_int ~ (age_c) * clust * sex +
        (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) +
        pub_z + changeage,
    data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long,
    na.action = na.exclude))
lin2 = (lmer(
    bpm_ext ~ (age_c) * clust * sex +
        (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) +
        pub_z + changeage,
    data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long,
    na.action = na.exclude))
poly1 = (lmer(
    bpm_int ~ (age_c + I(age_c^2)) * clust * sex +
        (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) +
        pub_z + changeage,
    data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long,
    na.action = na.exclude))
poly2 = (lmer(
    bpm_ext ~ (age_c + I(age_c^2)) * clust * sex +
        (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) +
        pub_z + changeage,
    data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long,
    na.action = na.exclude))

anova(lin1, poly1)
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)
## Data: agnes_res_env_bpm_long
## Models:
## lin1: bpm_int ~ (age_c) * clust * sex + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_z + changeage
## poly1: bpm_int ~ (age_c + I(age_c^2)) * clust * sex + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_z + changeage
##       npar    AIC    BIC logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## lin1    17 173454 173601 -86710    173420                         
## poly1   23 173151 173351 -86553    173105 314.28  6  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lin2, poly2)
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)
## Data: agnes_res_env_bpm_long
## Models:
## lin2: bpm_ext ~ (age_c) * clust * sex + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_z + changeage
## poly2: bpm_ext ~ (age_c + I(age_c^2)) * clust * sex + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_z + changeage
##       npar    AIC    BIC logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## lin2    17 159383 159530 -79674    159349                         
## poly2   23 159292 159491 -79623    159246 103.08  6  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Plot mental health trajectories

# mental health outcomes
# predicted values from the fitted models (not raw data)
custom_colors2 <- c("#E9C46A", "#36827F", "#464D77")

# if you want x-axis back in raw age units (instead of centered age)
age_mean <- mean(model.frame(mod1)$age, na.rm = TRUE)

# ---------- internalizing (predicted) ----------
pred_int <- ggpredict(mod1, terms = c("age_c [all]", "clust", "sex")) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(
    sex = recode(as.character(facet), `1` = "Male", `2` = "Female"),
    Group = factor(group, levels = c("1","2","3"),
                   labels = c("Low risk", "High trauma\n& familial risk", "High disadvantage")),
    sex = factor(sex, levels = c("Male", "Female")),
    age = x + age_mean   # comment this line out if you want centered age on x-axis
  )

int_sex <- ggplot(pred_int, aes(x = age, y = predicted, color = Group, fill = Group, linetype = Group)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors2) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1, 5.5)) +
  facet_wrap(~ sex, ncol = 1) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Internalizing Symptoms") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("solid", "solid", "solid"))

# ---------- externalizing (predicted) ----------
pred_ext <- ggpredict(mod2, terms = c("age_c [all]", "clust", "sex")) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(
    sex = recode(as.character(facet), `1` = "Male", `2` = "Female"),
    Group = factor(group, levels = c("1","2","3"),
                   labels = c("Low risk", "High trauma\n& familial risk", "High disadvantage")),
    sex = factor(sex, levels = c("Male", "Female")),
    age = x + age_mean   # comment this line out if you want centered age on x-axis
  )

ext_sex <- ggplot(pred_ext, aes(x = age, y = predicted, color = Group, fill = Group, linetype = Group)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors2) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1.25, 4)) +
  facet_wrap(~ sex, ncol = 1) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Externalizing Symptoms") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("solid", "solid", "solid"))

ggarrange(int_sex, ext_sex, nrow = 1, ncol = 2, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "right")

Risk and protective factors predicting mental health

# -----------------------------
# 0) Prep data
# -----------------------------
env <- colnames(agnes_res_env[3:32])

agnes_res_env_bpm_long_riskres <- agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>%
  left_join(
    agnes_res_env %>% dplyr::select(src_subject_id, all_of(env)),
    by = "src_subject_id"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    clust = as.factor(clust),
    sex   = as.factor(sex)
  )

agnes_res_env_bpm_long_riskres[env] <- scale(agnes_res_env_bpm_long_riskres[env])

# -----------------------------
# 1) Fit one env model and extract coefficient row
# -----------------------------
fit_env_row <- function(data, outcome, env_var, clust_id) { 
  f <- as.formula(paste0(
    outcome, " ~ (age_c + I(age_c^2)) * sex + `", env_var, "` + ",
    "changeage + pub_z + ",
    "(1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l)"
  )) 
  
  m <- lmer(f, data = data, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) 
  sm <- as.data.frame(summary(m)$coefficients, check.names = FALSE) 
  
  sm$term <- rownames(sm) 
  
  out <- sm %>% 
    mutate(term_clean = gsub("`", "", term)) %>%   # remove backticks from rownames for matching
    filter(term_clean == env_var) %>% 
    slice(1) %>% 
    select(-term_clean) 
  
  names(out)[names(out) == "Std. Error"] <- "Std..Error" 
  names(out)[names(out) == "t value"] <- "t.value" 
  
  out$env <- env_var 
  out$clust <- clust_id 
  out$outcome <- outcome 
  out$converged <- TRUE 
  out 
}

# -----------------------------
# 2) Run screening for one outcome
# -----------------------------
run_env_screen <- function(outcome_name) {
  bind_rows(lapply(levels(agnes_res_env_bpm_long_riskres$clust), function(k) {
    d_k <- agnes_res_env_bpm_long_riskres %>% filter(clust == k)

    bind_rows(lapply(env, function(v) fit_env_row(d_k, outcome_name, v, k)))
  })) %>%
    group_by(clust) %>%
    mutate(padj = p.adjust(`Pr(>|t|)`, method = "fdr")) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(padj = round(padj, 3))
}

# -----------------------------
# 3) Run for internalizing and externalizing
# -----------------------------
mh_env_summ_int <- run_env_screen("bpm_int")
mh_env_summ_ext <- run_env_screen("bpm_ext")

mh_env_summ_int
## # A tibble: 90 × 11

##  1   0.529      0.0219 8620.   24.1   1.10e-124 `Negative l… Nega… 1     bpm_int

Plot individual environmental measure

# ============================================================
# Plotting
# ============================================================

# -----------------------------
# 4) Split into protective vs risk
# -----------------------------
protective_env_int <- mh_env_summ_int %>% dplyr::filter(Estimate < 0, padj < 0.05)
protective_env_ext <- mh_env_summ_ext %>% dplyr::filter(Estimate < 0, padj < 0.05)

risk_env_int <- mh_env_summ_int %>% dplyr::filter(Estimate > 0, padj < 0.05)
risk_env_ext <- mh_env_summ_ext %>% dplyr::filter(Estimate > 0, padj < 0.05)

# -----------------------------
# 5) Add env domain groups + shorten labels (COI + other long labels)
# -----------------------------
varnames <- env

env_group_map <- c(
  stats::setNames(rep("child",        3), varnames[1:3]),
  stats::setNames(rep("parent",       4), varnames[4:7]),
  stats::setNames(rep("family",       6), varnames[8:13]),
  stats::setNames(rep("school",       5), varnames[14:18]),
  stats::setNames(rep("community",    5), varnames[19:23]),
  stats::setNames(rep("neighborhood", 7), varnames[24:30])
)

env_group_cols <- c(
  child        = viridisLite::viridis(6, direction = -1)[1],
  parent       = viridisLite::viridis(6, direction = -1)[2],
  family       = viridisLite::viridis(6, direction = -1)[3],
  school       = viridisLite::viridis(6, direction = -1)[4],
  community    = viridisLite::viridis(6, direction = -1)[5],
  neighborhood = viridisLite::viridis(6, direction = -1)[6]
)

plot_dfs <- c("protective_env_int", "protective_env_ext", "risk_env_int", "risk_env_ext")

for (nm in plot_dfs) {
  tmp <- get(nm)

  tmp <- tmp %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      env_label = env,
      env_label = gsub("Child opportunity index \\(education\\)", "COI (education)", env_label),
      env_label = gsub("Child opportunity index \\(health\\)", "COI (health)", env_label),
      env_label = gsub("Child opportunity index \\(social/economic\\)", "COI (soc/econ)", env_label),
      env_label = gsub("^Neighborhood safety$", "Neigh. safety", env_label),
      env_label = gsub("^Neighborhood disadvantage$", "Neigh. disadvantage", env_label),
      env_label = gsub("^Parent psychopathology$", "Parent psych.", env_label),
      env_label = gsub("^Other parent psychopathology$", "Other parent psych.", env_label),
      env_label = gsub("^Family int/cultural engagement$", "Fam. int/cultural", env_label),
      env_label = gsub("^Protective school environment$", "Protective school env.", env_label),
      env_group = unname(env_group_map[env]),
      env_group = factor(env_group, levels = c("child", "parent", "family", "school", "community", "neighborhood"))
    )

  assign(nm, tmp)
}

clust_labs <- c(
  "1" = "Low risk",
  "2" = "High trauma & familial risk",
  "3" = "High disadvantage"
)

protective_env_int <- protective_env_int %>%
  dplyr::mutate(clust = factor(clust, levels = c("1","2","3"), labels = unname(clust_labs)))

protective_env_ext <- protective_env_ext %>%
  dplyr::mutate(clust = factor(clust, levels = c("1","2","3"), labels = unname(clust_labs)))

risk_env_int <- risk_env_int %>%
  dplyr::mutate(clust = factor(clust, levels = c("1","2","3"), labels = unname(clust_labs)))

risk_env_ext <- risk_env_ext %>%
  dplyr::mutate(clust = factor(clust, levels = c("1","2","3"), labels = unname(clust_labs)))

# -----------------------------
# 6) Prepare faceted labels/order
# -----------------------------
protective_env_int <- protective_env_int %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    env_label_faceted = paste(env_label, clust, sep = "_"),
    env_label_faceted = forcats::fct_reorder(env_label_faceted, Estimate, .desc = TRUE)
  )

protective_env_ext <- protective_env_ext %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    env_label_faceted = paste(env_label, clust, sep = "_"),
    env_label_faceted = forcats::fct_reorder(env_label_faceted, Estimate, .desc = TRUE)
  )

risk_env_int <- risk_env_int %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    env_label_faceted = paste(env_label, clust, sep = "_"),
    env_label_faceted = forcats::fct_reorder(env_label_faceted, Estimate)
  )

risk_env_ext <- risk_env_ext %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    env_label_faceted = paste(env_label, clust, sep = "_"),
    env_label_faceted = forcats::fct_reorder(env_label_faceted, Estimate)
  )

# -----------------------------
# 7) Plot helper (colored by env domain)
# -----------------------------
make_env_plot <- function(df, ylab_text) {
  ggplot(df, aes(x = env_label_faceted, y = Estimate, color = env_group)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_errorbar(
      aes(ymin = Estimate - 1.96 * Std..Error, ymax = Estimate + 1.96 * Std..Error),
      width = 0.2
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = env_group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("_.*", "", x)) +
    facet_wrap(~ clust, scales = "free") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(
      x = "Environmental Variable",
      y = ylab_text,
      color = "Domain"
    ) +
    theme_classic()
}

# -----------------------------
# 8) Build plots
# -----------------------------
resilience_int <- make_env_plot(protective_env_int, "Adjusted association with Internalizing Symptoms")
resilience_ext <- make_env_plot(protective_env_ext, "Adjusted association with Externalizing Symptoms")

risk_int <- make_env_plot(risk_env_int, "Adjusted association with Internalizing Symptoms")
risk_ext <- make_env_plot(risk_env_ext, "Adjusted association with Externalizing Symptoms")

# -----------------------------
# 9) Combine
# -----------------------------
risk <- ggpubr::ggarrange(risk_int, risk_ext, nrow = 2, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "right")
res  <- ggpubr::ggarrange(resilience_int, resilience_ext, nrow = 2, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "right")

risk

res
